2000-10-01.title: A Virtual Battlefield Helps Keep Japan Alert
2000-10-01.abstract: Computerized war games are as close as postwar Japanese troops have ever come to actual conflict since end of World War II, when country formally renounced military aggression and limited its armed forces to self-defense; Japan's self-defense forces are one of most technologically advanced militaries in world, thanks to country's scientific know-how and generous budget; photo; map (M)

2000-10-01.title: Pakistan Newspaper Inspection Is Seen as Effort at Intimidation
2000-10-01.abstract: Civil libertarians and journalists in Pakistan react angrily and warily to surprise government inspection of electrical hookups at hedquarters of Dawn Group of Newspapers; visit by engineers and armed soldiers is presumed to be effort by military government to intimidate press; strong protests about news coverage have become recent staple of military government, which seized power in bloodless coup year ago (M)

2000-10-01.title: Insider´s Unflinching Look at Corsican Rebels
2000-10-01.abstract: GIANNUCCIO, Corsica — The setting is reassuring enough: a weathered stone house near a mountaintop on the island of Corsica, the Mediterranean glistening in the distance. Behind the windows are lace curtains and sepia portraits of grandparents on the wall.

2000-10-01.title: Serbian Opposition Says Strike Will Close Belgrade on Monday
2000-10-01.abstract: BELGRADE, Serbia, Oct. 1 -- Pressing a strategy of legal challenges and popular pressure to get President Slobodan Milosevic to concede that he lost last weekend's elections, Serbia's opposition vowed to shut Belgrade and the country down on Monday in what would be the first general strike in this country since World War II.

2000-10-01.title: Death Toll Rises in Mideast, Dimming Prospects for Peace
2000-10-01.abstract: JERUSALEM -- Rock-throwing Palestinians battled Israeli troops for a fourth day, striking Arabs shut down shops and schools, and leaders on both sides traded recriminations Sunday amid the worst surge of violence in four years.

2000-10-02.title: Wartime Slaves Use U.S. Law to Sue Japanese
2000-10-02.abstract: Thousands of old Chinese men who served as forced laborers for Japanese companies during World War II have filed landmark lawsuit in California asking for apology and compensation; are hoping US court system will provide millions of Chinese enslaved by Japan with sense of justice and international and historical recognition, Asian politics and regional judicial systems have so far failed to provide; are suing under 1999 California law that allows victims unable to pursue claims elsewhere to sue in California state courts against companies that may have benefitted from slave labor years ago and now have subsidiaries in US; cases of Wen Jinhan and Wang Songlin, who worked as wartime slaves for Japanese companies in Shenyang, discussed; map; photo (M)

2000-10-02.title: Clinton's Chance to Broker Pact May Be Gone
2000-10-02.abstract: Diplomats and other experts say prospects that Pres Clinton can broker end to Israeli-Palestinian conflict before he leaves office has all but evaporated with outbreak of fresh wave of violence in West Bank and Gaza over past four days; several pronounce peace effort virtually dead because of shattering of trust (M)

2000-10-02.title: Yugoslav Opposition Mobilizes
2000-10-02.abstract: BELGRADE, Serbia, Oct. 2 — As a general strike hit much of Serbia, Yugoslav President Slobodan Milosevic went on national television today to implore his citizens to reject the designs of what he called a traitorous opposition and to reelect him.

2000-10-02.title: 209 Years Later, the English Get American-Style Bill of Rights
2000-10-02.abstract: Britain will adopt first domestic bill of rights on October 2 in fiercely debated move likely to fundamentally reshape centuries-old relationship between citizens and government; change comes with scheduled enforcement of Human Rights Act, centerpiece of Labor government's legislative program, which for first time incorporates European Convention on Human Rights into English law; will give England written bill of rights enshrined in single document and containing same sort of guarantees Americans have had since 1791; Britain, which lacks written constitution, bestows on citizens so-called negative rights, under which they are allowed to do anything they want unless there is law specifically forbidding it (M)

2000-10-02.title: A Young Symbol of Mideast Violence
2000-10-02.abstract: 12-year-old Palestinian Muhammad al-Durrah, who was shot to death in croossfire between Israeli troops and Palestinian security forces in Gaza, becomes potent new symbol of what angry Palestinians contend is their continued victimization by Israeli occupiers; his death and critical wounding of his father was filmed at close range by French television crew and shown repeatedly in news programs throughout Middle East, including Israel; photo (S)

2000-10-03.title: At Arms Again, Suddenly
2000-10-03.abstract: News analysis; outbreak of worst Israeli-Palestinian violence in West Bank and Gaza since 1996 shows how quickly peacekmaking relationship can give way to basest, barest ethnic and religious animosities, jeopardizing so much of what has been established during last seven years; has left everything unclear, such as how long conflict will last and what toll will be on human level as well as politically and diplomatically; photo (M)

2000-10-03.title: Drought Portends Famine for Tajikistan
2000-10-03.abstract: Worst drought in half century threatens half of Tajikistan's population of six million with famine; poorest of newly independent states of former Soviet Union has suffered decade of nearly total industrial collapse, and is living in fragile peace threatened by Islamic extremism, relentless drug trafficking coursing out of Afghanistan, as well as by corruption at home; maps; photo (M)

2000-10-03.title: Gun Battle Halts Mideast Cease-Fire
2000-10-03.abstract: JERUSALEM, Oct. 3 - A cease-fire between Israel and the Palestinians crumbled within hours today when intense rioting and gun battles flared once more throughout the West Bank and Gaza Strip and in Arab towns inside Israel.

2000-10-03.title: Strikes Threaten Milosevic´s Power
2000-10-03.abstract: BELGRADE, Serbia, Oct. 3 — The Serbian government issued a harsh warning to the democratic opposition today, promising "special measures" and prosecutions against those organizing a spreading general strike in Serbia intended to oust Yugoslav President Slobodan Milosevic.

2000-10-03.title: Parties Squabble on 10th Anniversary Eve : Germans Trade Barbs Over Who Owns Unity
2000-10-03.abstract: FRANKFURT—  On the eve of Germany's 10th anniversary of reunification, Berlin's political debate has degenerated into a finger-pointing squabble over who deserves credit or blame for the monumental events of 1990.

2000-10-04.title: World Briefing
2000-10-04.abstract: EUROPE    SPAIN: TRUCKERS BLOCK BORDER TO FRANCE -- Spanish truckers blocked the two main border crossings into France for the second day running as part of a wave of protests to press for cheaper fuel. Taxi drivers staged a 12-hour strike in most Spanish cities and fishermen along the southern Mediterranean coast stayed in port in separate actions intended to force the government to cut fuel taxes, which can account for up to 60 percent of the price at the pump.   Benjamin Jones (NYT)

2000-10-04.title: Arafat and Barak Reportedly Reach Deal to End Violence
2000-10-04.abstract: PARIS - Middle East peace talks in Paris agreed on Wednesday on steps to curb Arab-Israeli violence that has rocked the region for almost a week, an Israeli official said.

2000-10-04.title: Love Finds a Way in Iran: 'Temporary Marriage'
2000-10-04.abstract: Temporary marriage is one of oddest loopholes in Islamic system in Iran, where people of all classes and degrees of religiosity pride selves on finding loopholes; such contracts enable unmarried couples to avoid severe penalties for having sex, dating or even holding hands; contracts can last for as little as few minutes or long as 99 years; have never before been popular, but are now being discussed by odd mix of feminists, clerics and officials as way to legalize premarital sex for young population forced to defer marriage; photos (M)

2000-10-04.title: Crowds Fill Montreal Streets for Pierre Trudeau's Funeral
2000-10-04.abstract: Canada holds state funeral for former Prime Minister Pierre Elliott Trudeau in Montreal, ending five days of national mourning; scene described; photo (M)

2000-10-04.title: Suharto's Son Admits Guilt In Maneuver To Stay Free
2000-10-04.abstract: Former Indonesian Pres Suharto's son, Hutomo Mandala Putra, who is known as Tommy Suharto, admits he is guilty of corruption by asking for presidential pardon to avoid serving 18-month prison term, meeting with prosecutors; his lawyers say they do not expect Pres Abdurrahman Wahid to grant pardon, but that Hutomo will remain free to pursue appeal during three months it takes to process clemency bid; constitutional scholars score government's failure to detain him (M)

2000-10-05.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-10-05.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: ACCORD ON NAZI LOOT -- Deputy Treasury Secretary Stuart E. Eizenstat said the United States and Russia had reached extensive agreement on opening archives to help find property looted by the Nazis. He declined to give details before addressing a conference this week in Vilnius, Lithuania, on Holocaust restitution. Nazi booty totaled about 220,000 artworks, according to estimates by the State Department, of which 60,000 have been recovered.   (Reuters)

2000-10-05.title: Moscow Journal; So the News Is Ho-Hum. The Show's Spectacular.
2000-10-05.abstract: Television program The Naked Truth, 13 minutes of usually sober and straight-faced newscasting by naked young women, is hit in Russia, where it has established channel M1 as home of off-beat and unexpected; Sergei L Moskovin, general director of M1's owner, Tele-express, and show's creator, comments; photos (M)

2000-10-05.title: Accord Ends 3 Days of Highway Blockades by Spanish Truckers
2000-10-05.abstract: Spanish truckers end strike, dismantling blockades at border crossings, after reaching accord on concessions to offset rising fuel prices (M)

2000-10-05.title: NO DEAL REACHED IN TALKS TO QUELL MIDEAST FIGHTING
2000-10-05.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak, Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat and State Sec Madeleine K Albright fail to agree on plan to end continuing Israeli-Palestinian clashes in talks, Paris; Barak heads home, declining invitation from Pres Hosni Mubarak to host further talks in Egypt; Albright plans to meet with Mubarak and Arafat; Paris talks also involved French Pres Jacques Chirac and United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan; major sticking point reportedly was how probe should be conducted into fighting, with Barak resisting Arafat's demand for international probe; violence spreads to Cairo, where some 1,500 students protesting Palestinian deaths stone supermarket run by reportedly Jewish-owned British company; Syrian students stone United States Embassy, Damascus; Jordanian police use tear gas to disperse some 800 people who try to march on Israeli Embassy, Amman (M)

2000-10-05.title: Mexico Ends Asylum Case, Sends Official Back to Cuba
2000-10-05.abstract: Mexico arrests and deports to Cuba Pedro Riera Escalante, Cuban intelligence official who broke with Castro government and sought political asylum (M)

2000-10-06.title: Barak and Arafat Order Their Forces to Pull Back
2000-10-06.abstract: Deadly clashes in Israel and Palestinian territories slow but do not stop, even as Palestinian and Israeli leaders issue orders to curb violence and both sides continue talks on how to end rioting that has spilled over region for a week; after stormy meeting in Paris with State Sec Madeleine K Albright fails to reach cease-fire agreement, Prime Min Ehud Barak and Yasir Arafat promise to order their commanders to pull back and restore calm to flash points under their control; Albright flies to Egypt to meet with Pres Hosni Mubarak, who is host for negotiating session with Albright and Arafat; Barak, after initially indicating that he would join talks, stays home, apparently to watch over events in Israel and Palestinian territories; photos (L)

2000-10-06.title: Hurricane Fades as It Hits Mexico's Gulf Coast
2000-10-06.abstract: Hurricane that has lashed four nations for a week strikes Mexico's gulf coast with 90-mile-an-hour winds, after killing at least a dozen people, driving tens of thousands from their homes and causing close to a quarter of a billion dollars in damage; map (S)

2000-10-06.title: Serbian Opposition Supporters Revel in the Streets of Belgrade
2000-10-06.abstract: BELGRADE, Yugoslavia -- As euphoric, all-night street celebrations of Slobodan Milosevic's apparent political demise wound down, Russia on Friday sent its foreign minister on a delicate mission said to include planned talks with the Yugoslav president in his secret hideout.

2000-10-06.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-10-06.abstract: EUROPE    FRANCE: GIRLS, 12, TO GET THE PILL  The National Assembly voted to allow school nurses to dispense morning-after contraceptive pills to girls as young as 12, with or without parental approval. The measure was part of a larger bill that would also allow pharmacists to sell the contraceptive without a prescription, even to minors. Officials hope the easy availability of the pills, which must be taken within 72 hours after intercourse, will help reduce the abortion rate among teens.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)    HUNGARY: DNA TESTS IDENTIFY EX-PRISONER  Genetic tests have established the identity of a former prisoner of war who returned to Hungary this summer after spending 53 years in a Russian mental hospital. DNA tests prove he is Andras Toma, left, from Sulyanbokor, eastern Hungary, said Dr. Andras Veer, director of the National Psychiatric and Neurological Institute in Budapest. ''For Andras Toma, World War II ended today,'' Dr. Veer said.    (AP)

2000-10-06.title: Irish Agency Knew of Risks In Blood Sales, Tribunal Says
2000-10-06.abstract: Official inquiry into infection of more than 200 Irish hemophiliacs with HIV and hepatitis C confirms that government's Blood Transfusion Service Board knowingly put them at risk throughout early 1980's; testimony to a tribunal by former execs and medical experts in recent weeks shows that organization sold infected blood products like clotting agents to Irish hospitals even when pharmaceutical companies that made products expressed serious concern about whether they were safe to use (M)

2000-10-07.title: Hole in Ozone Layer Stretches Over Chile
2000-10-07.abstract: WELLINGTON, New Zealand, Oct. 6 — The hole in the ozone layer over Antarctica stretched over a Chilean city when it ballooned to a record size last month, scientists reported this week.

2000-10-07.title: Lingerie That Loves the Limelight
2000-10-07.abstract: MILAN—  Four years ago, the designer Petra Pistoletto created for the Florence Biennale della Moda a pale pink and white sleeveless wedding gown replete with train.

2000-10-07.title: Abruptly, a U.S. Student In Mideast Turmoil's Grip
2000-10-07.abstract: Tuvia Grossman, 20-year-old Chicagoan studying at yeshiva in Jerusalem, says he and two friends were dragged from taxi by Palestinian youths and he was stabbed in leg and beaten by crowd with fists, feet and stones; says Israeli policeman drove crowd off and got him to hospital; photo of his bloodied face, in The New York Times on Sept 30, erroneously identified him as Palestinian, based on wire service information; photo (M)

2000-10-07.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-10-07.abstract: EUROPE    GERMANY: ANOTHER SYNAGOGUE ATTACK  In the second attack of its kind this week, stones were thrown at a synagogue in the Kreuzberg district of Berlin, breaking several windows. ''The broken windows are a mirror image on German society,'' said Andreas Nachama, head of Berlin's Jewish community. According to government figures, 817 anti-Semitic crimes were reported last year. Several politicians, including Foreign Minister Joschka Fischer, attended Sabbath services in Berlin to show solidarity with Jews.    Victor Homola (NYT)

2000-10-07.title: Keeping Shoes In the Family
2000-10-07.abstract: MILAN—  It was tourists from the beaches on the Adriatic coast coming inland to buy sandals from the local artisans that gave birth to San Mauro Pascoli's footwear industry. It eventually evolved into a production center of prestigious footwear, some of whose brands are known throughout the world.

2000-10-08.title: Disgraced Mexican Is Back, With Accusations of His Own
2000-10-08.abstract: Former Pres Carlos Salinas de Gortari returns to Mexico more than five years afer leaving in disgrace, and brings his 1,380-page memoir written during his Irish exile; book is directed at his hand-picked successor, Ernesto Zedillo, whom Salinas accuses of seeking political revenge by arresting his brother, Raul, and preparing arrest order against former president himself for supposedly obstructing investigation into assassination of Luis Donaldo Colosio, their party's presidential candidate in 1994; Salinas says he never asked his brother, Raul, for accounting of $90 million hidden in Swiss bank accounts; concedes that he has little credibility left among Mexicans; photo (M)

2000-10-08.title: A Document By Cuban Spy Talks of Acts Against C.I.A.
2000-10-08.abstract: Pedro Riera Escalante, Cuban spy arrested on run from his government by Mexican government, carried documents outlining his career running operations against Central Intelligence Agency station in Mexico City and other operations he ran in Europe and Africa; Riera's deportation to Cuba ended his monthlong effort to win political asylum in Mexico; Riera said he became deeply disillusioned with Pres Fidel Castro for turning Cuban intelligence agency to his political ends; United States officials refuse to comment on Riera's case, other than statement from American Embassy in Mexico City expressing concern about his fate in Cuba (M)

2000-10-08.title: Hostility Toward Israelis Resurfaces in a Wave of Violence
2000-10-08.abstract: There is anger churning across Middle East since violent clashes between Israelis and Palestinians began; polarization of Muslim opinion may sharply reduce prospects for overall peace settlement, even if violence can be curbed; angry crowds march in capitals like Baghdad and Tehran, hotbeds of hostility toward Israel, but also in nations that have moved in recent years toward acceptance of Israel's existence, including Kuwait and United Arab Emirates; there is also anger in Arab countries that have signed peace agreements with Israel, such as Jordan; photo if anti-Israel protesters in Saudi Arabia (M)

2000-10-08.title: How Yeltsin Nearly Scuttled Democracy in Russia
2000-10-08.abstract: Former Pres Boris N Yeltsin of Russia, in new memoir, says he almost decided in 1996 to upend democratic process in Russia by banning Communist Party, dissolving Parliament and postponing presidential elections he seemed certain at time to lose; credits his daughter, Tatyana B Dyachenko, and former Deputy Prime Min Anatoly B Chubais for convincing him that shredding Constitution with illegal grab for power would backfire; memoir is likely to rekindle debate about Yeltsin's contribution to democratic process in Russia after Soviet breakup in 1991 that he helped to engineer; book includes number of frank disclosures, including confession of his weakness for alcohol; photo (M)

2000-10-08.title: Neighborhood Opponents of Milosevic Testify to Wild Abuses by His Son
2000-10-08.abstract: Residents of Pozarevac say Slobodan Milosevic's son, Marko, left town at characteristic high speed in convoy of three jeeps, supposedly to flee with his wife and child to Russia; say Marko should have been prevented from leaving so he could stand trial for string of accusations ranging from drug-smuggling and racketeering to physical violence and political repression; local members of student resistance organization tell of being beaten up by Marko and his thugs; photo (M)

2000-10-09.title: Winning Belgrade
2000-10-09.abstract: Vojislav Kostunica, a constitutional lawyer born into a legal family that had a tradition of opposing the Communists, had always kept his distance from Slobodan Milosevic. It was his unpretentious and somehow normal air that made him a perfect candidate for the presidential election, which became a referendum on Mr. Milosevic himself.    Man in the News, Page A6.

2000-10-09.title: Once Again, Us vs. Them
2000-10-09.abstract: Ten days of violence between Palestinians and Israelis dissolves internal differences in both camps, reducing conflict to barest essence: Jew against Arab, Arab against Jew; many Israelis, especially doves, are staggered by swift tumble from what seemed to be the brink of a resolution back down into depths of an elemental ethnic hatred; there is no such disillusionment among Palestinians because for an overwhelming majority of them, the peace felt only skin-deep, not bone-deep, and very fragile; unity throughout Arab world has not been so strong for quite some time; photo (M)

2000-10-09.title: SmithKline Beecham to Buy Block Drug Company for $1.24 Billion
2000-10-09.abstract: LONDON -- SmithKline Beecham PLC is to buy U.S.-based Block Drug Co. for $1.24 billion, the pharmaceutical giant announced Monday. SmithKline Beecham has made a cash tender offer for all outstanding Block common stock at $53 per share, the drug group said in a statement. Block Drug, based in Jersey City, New Jersey, manufactures Sensodyne toothpaste, Poli-Grip denture adhesive and other consumer health care products. The firm reported sales of $864.3 million in the year to March 31, 2000. SmithKline said it had received commitments from a majority of Block shareholders to tender into the offer. "This acquisition will significantly enhance SB's Consumer Health Care business, which is a key part of out strategy for corporate growth," said SmithKline Beecham CEO J.P. Garnier. SmithKline Beecham sold $4.1 billion worth of consumer health care products in 1999, a third of its total sales. The deal places SmithKline, which already produces the Aquafresh brand, among the world's top three toothpaste manufacturers. The company said it was too early to say what effect the takeover would have on jobs at Block Drug, which employs 3,000 people "In any merger or takeover, jobs are lost," said SmithKline Beecham spokeswoman Lynne Smith. "We will have a process that ensures we keep the best people ... There will be an effect in both companies." The deal, which must gain regulatory approval in the United States and Europe, is not expected to affect SmithKline Beecham's proposed merger with Glaxo Wellcome PLC, the statement said. That merger, which also is awaiting U.S. and British regulatory approval, is scheduled to be completed by the end of the year.

2000-10-09.title: How Small Town Turned Out For Kostunica at a Key Time
2000-10-09.abstract: People of Cacak, Serbia, celebrate their part in revolt that forced Slobodan Milosevic from power; led by their mayor, Velimir Ilic, they were at core of tide of people who gathered in Belgrade and overran federal Parliament and state television station; photo (M)

2000-10-09.title: Argentine President Is Trying To Keep Coalition Together
2000-10-09.abstract: Argentine Government of Pres Fernando de la Rua is in crisis for failing to deliver on promises to jump-start the economy and cleanse political system corroding from corruption and public distrust; he shook up cabinet in wake of bribery investigation, but Carlos Alvarez resigned as vice president because two high-level officials implicated in the scandal were retained; photo (M)

2000-10-10.title: Zimbabwe Authorities Crack Down on Political Opposition
2000-10-10.abstract: Zimbabwean government arrests three opposition members of Parliament on charges of inciting violence and sprays tear gas on their supporters demonstrating in Harare; officials also vow to arrest opposition leader Morgan Tsvangirai on similar grounds; rising tensions reflect hardening of political lines just two months after Pres Robert Mugabe bowed to election results and welcomed an opposition into Parliament for first time (M)

2000-10-10.title: 3 Israelis Are Now Pawns in a Growing Deal
2000-10-10.abstract: Hezbollah's capture of three Israeli soldiers along Lebanese border was apparently well-planned operation intended to coincide with Palestinian protests in Jerusalem, and with aim of creating bargaining chip for release of at least 19 Islamic militants in Israeli jails; now Lebanon waits nervously to see if Israel will retaliate for the capture, and series of international envoys race frantically through meetings, seeking to ease tension on volatile border and arrange some kind of deal; photo (M)

2000-10-10.title: Sirimavo Bandaranaike, 84, Matriarch of Sri Lanka, Dies
2000-10-10.abstract: BANGALORE, India, Oct. 10 — Sirimavo Bandaranaike of Sri Lanka, the first woman in the world to serve as a prime minister, died today shortly after voting in Sri Lanka's elections. She was 84.

2000-10-10.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-10-10.abstract: ASIA    INDONESIA: MILITARY SHUFFLE -- President Abdurrahman Wahid appointed new chiefs of staff for the army and the navy as part of efforts to bring Indonesia's once all-powerful military under civilian control. Although no official reason was given for the shuffle, analysts and officials close to Mr. Wahid, left, said the move was needed to ensure that the Government had the full allegiance of the armed forces as it begins dismantling the military's illicit businesses and prosecuting officers for human rights abuses.   Calvin Sims (NYT)    INDIA: JAIL TERM FOR JAYALITHA -- Jayalitha Jayaram, the flamboyant former chief minister of Tamil Nadu, has been sentenced to three years in prison on corruption charges. Jayalitha, a former movie star known by her first name, was charged with buying a large tract of government land at a fraction of its value.     Barry Bearak (NYT)

2000-10-10.title: Russian Border Guard Is Killed in Afghan Frontier Skirmish
2000-10-10.abstract: MOSCOW, Oct. 10 — In a sign of escalating tension, a Russian border guard was killed and five other soldiers were wounded in a fire fight on Tajikistan's border with Afghanistan during the weekend, the Russian armed forces confirmed today.

2000-10-11.title: Leader Denies Woes Amount To a Crisis In Argentina
2000-10-11.abstract: Argentine Pres Fernando de la Rua denies recent political and economic problems amount to crisis; takes swipe at Carlos Alvarez, who resigned as vice president protesting that administration is not doing enough to fight corruption; also insists that cabinet shake-up will make his economic policies more effective (M)

2000-10-11.title: Sudan, at U.N., Fails to Win Council Seat
2000-10-11.abstract: Sudan's bid to win two-year term on UN Security Council is defeated after four rounds of voting; contested African seat finally goes to Mauritius; Colombia, Ireland, Norway and Singapore also win rotating seats (S)

2000-10-11.title: The Sleeping Beauties Of Couture Are Waking
2000-10-11.abstract: PARIS—  The success stories of Gucci and LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton and the high risks involved in Internet start-ups are prompting venture capitalists to turn to fashion as a long-term investment.

2000-10-11.title: On a TV Show, Jerusalem Is All Anger and Yelling (but Nobody Gets Hurt)
2000-10-11.abstract: Ted Koppel presides, or tries to preside, over raucous Nightline program broadcast from East Jerusalem, pitting angry Israelis against angry Palestinians (M)

2000-10-11.title: Clinton, After Courting Arafat, Feels Frustrated by Latest Turn
2000-10-11.abstract: Pres Clinton, who has lavished more attention on Yasir Arafat than on any world leader, feels exasperated and even betrayed as Palestinian leader turns aside entreaties to restore calm in Palestinian areas; Clinton has spoken to Arafat daily in last week, impressing on him, location by location, how he must bring rioting under control; officials, who say Arafat allowed violence to drift for his own advantage, finally began sounding worried, but still resists Clinton's request to issue statement to his people; he demands Israel issue parallel statement, and also apparently fears losing face if he fails; question of his control over rampaging discussed; Clinton's long courting of Arafat includes $300 million in US aid after Oslo peace accord and another $300 million after 1998 Wye accord (M)

2000-10-12.title: CLINTON TO SCRAP BELGRADE EMBARGO ON OIL AND TRAVEL
2000-10-12.abstract: Pres Clinton to lift many of trade and economic sanctions against Yugoslavia intended to speed fall of former Pres Slobodan Milosevic; will immediately end ban on American flights and oil embargo; will join with European Union in sweeping away variety of other sanctions over next few weeks, except for those aimed at barring Milosevic, his family and allies from sending assets abroad; will retain so-called 'outer wall' of sanctions' that bar World Bank and International Monetary Fund aid until United States discusses Milosevic's legal fate with Pres Vojislav Kostunica (M)

2000-10-12.title: In America, New Names Face an Uphill Struggle
2000-10-12.abstract: NEW YORK—  You're a young creative American fashion designer and you want to make your name. Should your first move be to catch a plane to Europe?

2000-10-12.title: Nobel Winner Laments Poverty of Russian Science
2000-10-12.abstract: Dr Zhores Ivanovich Alferov, Nobel Prize winner in physics, scores Russia's failure to spend enough money on basic scientific research, speech to Russian Parliament, where he is member of Communist faction; wins prize for work conducted almost entirely when Russia was under Communist rule and scientists were cherished; photo (M)

2000-10-12.title: Cilcain Journal; Farmer Who Sowed Wind That Rattled Britain
2000-10-12.abstract: Interview with farmer Brynie Williams, who led fuel price protest at refinery near his Cilcain, Wales, home that rapidly spread across Britain; he expresses surprise that his efforts helped undermine popularity of Prime Min Tony Blair; map; photo (M)

2000-10-12.title: Palestinians Kill 2 Israeli Soldiers in Police Custody
2000-10-12.abstract: RAMALLAH, West Bank, Oct. 12 -- Youths stormed a Palestinian police station in the West Bank on Thursday and killed two Israeli soldiers who were in custody, police sources and witnesses said.

2000-10-13.title: Five Bodies of U.S. Sailors Killed in Blast Flown to Germany
2000-10-13.abstract: RAMSTEIN AIR BASE, Germany, Oct. 13 — The remains of five American sailors, killed on Thursday during a terrorist attack on the U.S.S. Cole near Yemen, arrived here today aboard the first of several Air Force cargo planes that are evacuating the dead as well as the injured.

2000-10-13.title: Craze for Customization Tears Up the Rules
2000-10-13.abstract: PARIS—  Fashion is having a do-it-yourself moment. It seems everywhere you look, there's a brand or designer encouraging the customer to get involved in the design process: to tear your T-shirt apart, create your own running shoes, customize your cocktail dress, all in the name of individuality.

2000-10-13.title: Clinton Trip to North Korea Is Possible This Year
2000-10-13.abstract: White House says Pres Clinton may travel to North Korea later this year, in what would be first visit by American president to that country; Clinton trip, still tentative, would follow visit by Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright (M)

2000-10-13.title: Imbroglio Over the Elf-Kohl Payoff:Was It a Hoax to Hide Kickbacks?
2000-10-13.abstract: PARIS—  For three years, Europe has been buzzing about a massive secret payment allegedly made in 1992 by Elf Aquitaine, the French oil company, to the political slush fund of Chancellor Helmut Kohl of Germany. Elf officials have said that the payment was ordered by the late French president, Francois Mitterrand.

2000-10-13.title: Little by Little, Designers Take To Web Sales
2000-10-13.abstract: NEW YORK—  One in the morning: I am working late in the office, but fancy a spot of shopping. Bergdorf Goodman is closed, but Luxlook.com is open 24 hours a day, and I quickly find a flamboyant Versace tie with the designer's signature medallions for dollars 115. A few clicks, and the tie is ordered.

2000-10-14.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-10-14.abstract: EUROPE    FRANCE: MAYOR OUSTED FROM PARTY  Mayor Jean Tiberi of Paris, who has been trying to hold onto his job despite being the focus of several investigations into corruption and vote fixing, has been dealt another blow. Already forced to give up leadership of the local branch of the Rally for the Republic, he has now been ousted from the party altogether. Mr. Tiberi, who insisted he would run for re-election, said he had been ''humiliated and mistreated'' by his Gaullist party.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)    RUSSIA: KURSK THEORIES  Experts investigating the sinking of the submarine Kursk no longer believe a collision with a foreign submarine is the likely cause of the tragedy. No evidence of a collision was found during a search of the seabed floor, Deputy Prime Minister Ilya Klebanov said. Mr. Klebanov said that a collision had not been ruled out, but that other theories -- that the sub struck a World War II mine, or that a torpedo exploded -- appeared equally valid.      Michael Wines (NYT)

2000-10-14.title: Palestinians May Seek General Assembly Vote to Condemn Israel
2000-10-14.abstract: Palestinians, faced with United States vow to veto any Security Council resolution on fighting between Israel and Palestinians, weigh seeking special United Nations General Assembly session to condemn Israel; Amb Richard C Holbrooke says Pres Clinton made decision to veto any further resolutions, interview; meets with several Arab diplomats to discuss what role UN should play (M)

2000-10-14.title: 'Ghetto Fabulous' Glitter Fades
2000-10-14.abstract: NEW YORK—  Not so long ago in New York, the Gucci flagship reopened on Fifth Avenue with a grand celebration. The store had been renovated with great expense and folks were eager to see the revitalized building which also happened to be the Gucci empire's first home in the United States.

2000-10-14.title: Israel in Shock as It Buries Mob's Victim
2000-10-14.abstract: Israel buries Vadim Norzich, who along with fellow reserve soldier, Yossi Avrahami, was stabbed, beaten and burned to death by mob at Palestinian police station, Ramallah, after getting lost in West Bank; Israelis and Palestinians are in state of shock over incident and Israeli retaliatory attack, which have greatly dimmed hopes for peace; Israel, which prides itself on allowing freedom of worship at Al Aksa Mosque, Jerusalem, bars Muslims younger than 45 from attending prayer; photos (M)

2000-10-14.title: Bomb Jars Embassy, A Reminder Of Tense Tie
2000-10-14.abstract: Bomb rocks Britain's Yemen Embassy, jarring memories of Britain's uncomfortable ties with its former colony and of terrorist underworld that seems to be at work there; British experts comment (M)

2000-10-15.title: Ex-Pentagon Planner Defends Sending U.S. Ships to Yemen
2000-10-15.abstract: Retired Gen Anthony C Zinni, architect of plan to send United States Navy ships into port of Aden to improve ties with Yemen, holds he sought to keep Yemen from becoming 'rats' nest' of terrorist groups; says several planned ship visits were vetoed by Amb Barbara K Bodine, who worried about terrorist threat; notes he also sent American special forces to Yemen to train local military in counterterrorism (M)

2000-10-15.title: Falling Short of Peace
2000-10-15.abstract: Analysis of view that Clinton administration, now scrambling for new Israeli-Palestinian summit to arrange simple truce, may have erred in rushing Camp David peace talks in quest for grand peace accord; even administration officials now see little chance of restoring peace effort during Clinton's presidency; photo (M)

2000-10-15.title: Muslim Leader in Bosnia Retires
2000-10-15.abstract: SARAJEVO, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Oct. 14 — The Bosnian Muslim leader, Alija Izetbegovic, who led the resistance to the Serbs in the 1992-95 Bosnian war, stepped down today from the country's tripartite presidency, two years before his mandate was to expire.

2000-10-15.title: Specialists Scour U.S.S. Cole for Clues to Deadly Blast
2000-10-15.abstract: ADEN, Yemen -- The crew of the crippled USS Cole gathered on its listing deck Sunday to pay tribute to 17 shipmates killed in an apparent suicide bombing that also ripped into the U.S. Navy's strategic plans in the Middle East.

2000-10-15.title: Mugabe's Foes in Zimbabwe, Stymied, Talk of Violence
2000-10-15.abstract: Opposition joy over their sweep in June of over third of parliamentary seats in Zimbabwe has quickly dissolved into discontent as opposition legislators repeatedly find selves outnumbered and ignored; opposition is warning for first time that its supporters may turn to violence if Pres Robert Mugabe's government continues to block their efforts at change; map; photo (M)

2000-10-16.title: Protestant's Slipping Hold Imperils Ulster Peace Accord
2000-10-16.abstract: A showdown is shaping up within the dominant Protestant political party that could lead to the ouster of its longtime leader, David Trimble, and an end to the power-sharing government at the heart of Northern Ireland's peace accord.     ''This is more serious than it's ever been,'' Paul Bew, professor of Irish history at Queens University, said of the struggle to salvage the 1998 accord. ''Can the agreement be lost over the next few weeks? The answer sadly is yes, it's more likely to be lost than be saved.''

2000-10-16.title: Anti-Israeli Anger Swells Flow of Aid
2000-10-16.abstract: The doctor at the central blood bank advised Nagwa Omar not to donate because she is breast-feeding her fourth child. But the 37-year-old university professor was adamant.     ''I insist,'' Dr. Omar said, as she rolled up her sleeve today, offering her right arm to the nurse who would draw her blood. ''If I could even go to Palestine to fight, I would.''

2000-10-16.title: U.S. Suspends All Refueling in Yemen for Security Inquiry
2000-10-16.abstract: The Pentagon today suspended all naval refueling operations in Yemen until officials determine whether lax security in the port at Aden left the American destroyer Cole vulnerable to a presumed bombing attack that killed 17 sailors and wounded more than three dozen others.    The security at the port -- and with the private contractor that oversaw the refueling -- has become a focus of the widening investigation into what officials have called a suicide attack. Since the attack is believed to have involved a harbor boat helping the Cole moor at a refueling site in Aden's port, officials say they believe that those involved skillfully infiltrated the port's operations or the contractor itself.

2000-10-16.title: China's Premier, on Japan Visit, Wears a Friendly Face
2000-10-16.abstract: The last time a top Chinese leader came to Japan, he lectured at every stop over the nation's past war crimes, demanding an apology. The visit quickly degenerated into a fuming, finger-pointing session that damaged relations between the two countries, Asia's most powerful.    So there was a palpable sense of relief here this weekend when Prime Minister Zhu Rongji of China soft-pedaled the bitter question of Japan's conquest of his country in the 1930's as he made the rounds of official banquets and meetings with business groups and ordinary Japanese. He focused instead on economic and security issues whose resolution both countries say could bring them closer together.

2000-10-16.title: PRESIDENT HEADS TO MIDEAST TALKS HOPING FOR TRUCE
2000-10-16.abstract: Setting an urgent goal of a truce between Israelis and Palestinians, President Clinton headed today for a Middle East summit meeting in Egypt that aides described as potentially the most difficult of his presidency.     Instead of playing the role of triumphant peacemaker that he had hoped for just a month ago, Mr. Clinton will arrive in Egypt trying to quell the violence that has left more than 100 people dead and that the White House fears could spill trouble across the region.

2000-10-17.title: Clashes Continue as Palestinians Protest Summit Meeting
2000-10-17.abstract: Stone-throwing clashes punctuated by exchanges of gunfire erupt across West Bank and Gaza Strip as thousands of Palestinians take to streets in message of defiance over peace summit in Sharm el Sheik; Palestinians report 13-year-old boy and policeman killed in resurging violence; dozens of protestors are wounded; Israeli Army reports two soldiers wounded by gunfire; armed members of Yasir Arafat's Fatah movement are visible in many of dfemonstrations after group's leadership urges Palestinians to keep up wave of violent protests; photo (M)

2000-10-17.title: Liberian President Urges U.N. to Move Into Rebel-Held Areas in Sierra Leone
2000-10-17.abstract: Pres Charles Taylor of Liberia suggests to UN Security Council peace mission that peacekeeping troops be allowed to move into rebel-held diamond-producing areas of Sierra Leone while the rebels return to negotiations that were shattered in May by their breach of peace accord; some council members remain skeptical; say proposal may be tactical ploy by Taylor, who has been close to rebel's United Revolutionary Front but has denied widespread allegations that he is prospering from diamonds and arms trade with them (M)

2000-10-17.title: Case Goes to New York F.B.I. Office
2000-10-17.abstract: Federal Bureau of Investigation assigns its New York office to lead investigation into terrorist attack against US destroyer Cole in Yemen (S)

2000-10-17.title: Currency Nears Its Low As ECB Leader Seems Hesitant on Intervention : New Doubts On Support Push Euro Downward
2000-10-17.abstract: FRANKFURT—  Europe's common currency slid back toward its record low Monday after Wim Duisenberg, president of the European Central Bank, appeared to cast doubt on the commitment of central banks to intervene anew in support of the euro.

2000-10-17.title: Leaders in the Middle East Agree to Seek a Cease Fire
2000-10-17.abstract: SHARM EL-SHEIK, Egypt (AP) -- Israel and the Palestinians agreed Tuesday to end the street violence that has torn their region apart for more than two weeks and to get back on the path toward a permanent solution to their conflict.

2000-10-18.title: Emerging From Its Crisis, East Timor Rebuilds
2000-10-18.abstract: East Timor, destroyed by rampaging militias after its Aug vote for independence from Indonesia, is struggling to rebuild and forge national identity; United Nations, which saved nation from vandals, will continue to run it for year and help put in place basic structures of nationhood; process, after slow start, is well under way, with aid reaching people, job programs functioning and policies being made; local leaders have begun to squabble over political power; map; photo (M)

2000-10-18.title: 2 SIDES IN MIDEAST PROMISE TO WORK TO STOP VIOLENCE
2000-10-18.abstract: Pres Clinton, after intensive summit meeting in Sharm el Sheik, Egypt, with Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, says they have agreed to work to stop violence in West Bank and Gaza and to try to bar it from escalating; warns of difficulties ahead; cease-fire accord calls for Israel to reopen Gaza airport, end border closings and withdraw its forces stationed at edge of Palestinian territories; calls for Palestinians to act to stop riots and gun battles, crack down on incitement against Israel and rearrest Islamic fundamentists freed from prison last week; some West Bank Palestinians complain Arafat, who left Egypt quickly to return to Gaza, was railroaded into accepting watered-down compromises; sporadic gun battles continue in West Bank and Gaza Strip as Palestinian militants reject call to end fighting; accord returns United States to its former role as broker, policeman and judge, role Barak sought to end when he took office; Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak admits accord will disappoint many; Palestinians are particularly disappointed that accord does not mandate international inquiry into recent violence; American-led fact-finding committee will instead work in consultation with United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan; photos (M)

2000-10-18.title: Manila Journal; This Whistle-Blower Could Topple a President
2000-10-18.abstract: Gov Luis Singson, who threatens to bring down Philippines government with charge that Pres Joseph Estrada accepted over $8 million in kickbacks from illegal gambling, sounds more like cheerful accomplice than angry avenger, interview; describes Estrada's gambling habits; ex-Pres Corazon C Aquino joins calls for Estrada's resignation; photos (M)

2000-10-18.title: For Clinton, the Suspense Is Not Over
2000-10-18.abstract: Pres Clinton played pivotal role at Israeli-Palestinian summit conference in devising mechanism to defuse deadly eruption of violence; his personality and ability to connect with Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat reportedly provided constant thread in talks; his back-to-back, separate sessions with Arafat and Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak were key bridge between two men no longer able to meet face to face (M)

2000-10-18.title: U.S. Aid to Colombia Worries Hemisphere's Defense Leaders
2000-10-18.abstract: Concern over United States military aid to Colombia to fight drug traffickers and guerrillas who protect them overshadows formal agenda as Western Hemisphere defense ministers assemble, Manaus, Brazil, for meetings aimed at strengthening regional military and security cooperation; US Sec William Cohen and Colombian Sec Luis Fernando Ramirez Acuna are on hand to allay concerns; Cohen insists US will not become involved militarily; leaders of bordering nations fear effort will push coca cultivation, refugees and fighting onto their territories; Cohen warns any effort to ignore crisis or stay aloof from it is likely to backfire (M)

2000-10-19.title: Balkans After Milosevic: Still Perilous Waters
2000-10-19.abstract: Analysis of treacherous problems that abound in Balkan region even after removal of Slobodan Milosevic in Serbia; Vojislav Kostunica, new Yugoslav president, has made conciliatory signals toward Croatia, which long battled Belgrade for its independence, and Montenegro, where secessionist currents are strong; Kostunica's past nationalism makes some neighbors skeptical, and his popularity in West makes other neighbors envious; his arrival has come so late in process of Yugoslav disintegration that it is far from clear that process can be arrested; American officials say Washington's twin priorities are consolidating Serbia's democratic transition and encouraging Kostunica to reassure Serbia's neighbors; photos (M)

2000-10-19.title: Bodies of 6 Remind Italy of Migrants' Plight
2000-10-19.abstract: Bodies of six illegal immigrants, believed to have suffocated in truck, are found dumped on side of highway in southern Italy; it is grim reminder of perils of trying to immigrate to Europe; Italy is major port of entry to Europe for desperate would-be immigrants from Albania, Turkey, Iraq and Africa; photo (M)

2000-10-19.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-10-19.abstract: EUROPE    ITALY: VOTING ABROAD -- A constitutional amendment was approved to allow Italians overseas to elect their own legislators, including candidates who live abroad. Twelve seats in the 630-seat lower house and six in the 315-seat Senate are to be allocated to representatives of Italians abroad, of whom there are about 4 million, with more than 300,000 in the United States and Canada. First, however, a law must be passed setting up candidates' procedural rules. Voting by absentee ballot is not allowed.    Alessandra Stanley (NYT)    FRANCE: CABINET RESIGNATION -- Martine Aubry, the cabinet minister who shepherded the introduction of the government's 35-hour workweek policy, has quit her cabinet post to run for mayor of Lille. The widely expected move is seen as a steppingstone to run for higher office in 2002. If Prime Minister Lionel Jospin, who is expected to run for president in 2002, were to win, Mrs. Aubry could well replace him as prime minister. Mr. Jospin named his justice minister, Elisabeth Guigou, to take over Mrs. Aubry's portfolio.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)

2000-10-19.title: Living Under Siege in Israeli Bunker
2000-10-19.abstract: Israeli soldiers at army outpost at Kfar Darom in Gaza strip live under siege; they do not leave heavily guarded outpost unless packed inside armored jeeps; Palestinians frequently hurl stones and gasoline bombs into outpost, which protects small Israeli settlement; Israeli soldiers in Gaza complain that they have been ordered to restrain their fire and are often held back from putting down protests; Palestinians contend Israeli troops use excessive force to quell disturbances; photos; map (M)

2000-10-19.title: Ship Bomb Inquiry Focuses on Safe Houses and Deserted Car
2000-10-19.abstract: Yemeni and American teams investigating bombing of destroyer Cole center their inquiry on two safe houses and car found abandoned across harbor from where ship was attacked, killing 17 sailors; Aden's main newspaper reports that bomb-making equipment was found in at least one of houses, and that traces of explosives were found in car; chief of Saudi Arabia's intelligence services, Prince Turki bin al-Faisal, arrrives in Aden, apparently to help with investigation; Turki has monitored leftist and Islamic radical groups for last two decades; recovery of 8 more bodies leaves 4 of 17 dead sailors from USS Cole still unaccounted for; maps (M)

2000-10-20.title: Illegal Migrants Bound for U.S. Found in Box at Hong Kong Port
2000-10-20.abstract: Hong Kong customs agents discover 26 illegal Chinese migrants hidden in shipping container bound for Long Beach, Calif (M)

2000-10-20.title: Bank Chief Admits Euro Gaffe As Currency Hovers Near Low : Duisenberg Acts To Limit Damage
2000-10-20.abstract: PARIS—  Wim Duisenberg, the president of the European Central Bank, acknowledged Thursday that a loose-lipped blunder had undermined the euro, but he answered questions about whether he would resign with a terse "no."

2000-10-20.title: Possible Lapses in Security At Yemen Port Investigated
2000-10-20.abstract: Defense Department announces that investigation into possible security lapses in connection with US warships visiting Yemen will be focus of independent investigation led by two former commanders, Adm Harold W Gehman and Gen William Crouch; Gen Anthony C Zinni, who retired as commander of US forces in the region in August, takes responsibility for having chosen Yemen as refueling stop, calling it safer and more strategically placed than other ports he had considered; photo (M)

2000-10-20.title: U.S. Says It Was Warned On Egyptian Islamic Group
2000-10-20.abstract: US intelligence officials say they received reports in late May that militant Egyptian group Islamic Jihad was in final stages of preparing a terror attack against US targets, and that they passed on warning to all US posts and foreign governments in Middle East; Islamic Jihad, a faction of which is closely associated with Osama bin Laden, is under scrutiny as possible culprit in bombing of USS Cole in Yemen (M)

2000-10-20.title: Belgrade Journal; From the Wings, a Prince Takes the Stage in Serbia
2000-10-20.abstract: Crown Prince Alexander, heir to Yugoslav throne, is making tour of Serbia to make it clear that he intends to help his country now that Slobodan Milosevic, whom he openly criticized, has left power; Alexander meets with Pres Kostunica, Serbian Orthodox Church leader Patriarch Pavle, and opposition groups; promises to return to live in his homeland, but brushes off questions of a return to monarchy; photo (Belgrade Journal) (M)

2000-10-21.title: U.N. Assembly Adopts Resolution Condemning Israel for an 'Excessive Use of Force'
2000-10-21.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan asks General Assembly to make 'real difference' by helping bring end to fighting between Israel and Palestinians; Assembly later discusses and adopts resolution condemning Israel for 'excessive use of force' against Palestinians; urges both sides to return to negotiating table (M)

2000-10-21.title: Masterpieces From an Unparalleled Collection : A Treasure Trove From Japan
2000-10-21.abstract: NEW YORK—  It is every museum director's dream.

2000-10-21.title: John Worsley, 81, Artist Whose Wartime Creation Outfoxed the Nazis
In Lean Times for Chinese Art, Dealers Triumph Over Auctioneers
2000-10-21.abstract: NEW YORK—  Last September, a marked shift in the balance of commercial power between auction houses and the art trade could be observed in the international scene within a specific area that has never had it so good — Chinese art.

2000-10-21.title: Mideast States Urged to Use An Arms Pact To Build Trust
2000-10-21.abstract: Jose M Bustani, director of Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons, makes plea in UN General Assembly to Israel, Egypt, Palestinians and others in Middle East to adhere to treaty banning use of such weapons as way to reduce tensions in region (M)

2000-10-22.title: New Leader Of Yugoslavia Due in Bosnia
2000-10-22.abstract: New Yugoslav leader Vojislav Kostunica will visit Sarajevo, first such visit by Yugoslav president since Bosnia declared independence in 1992 (S)

2000-10-22.title: Chechnya Caught In Unending Limbo Of War and Peace
2000-10-22.abstract: Military violence remains part of daily routine of Grozny, Chechnya, months after Russia declared conclusion of the military phase of 'counterterrorist' operation; city is caught between a war that Russians cannot end and a peace that cannot take hold until a new Chechen leadership emerges to tame or supplant the warlords and 'bandits' whom Moscow is determined to crush; 150,000 people who have returned to live in Grozny complain that they live in repressive conditions of undeclared martial law, subjected to random searches, arrests, extortion and intimidation at more than 100 checkpoints; about 7,000 Russian troops are stationed in city, choking movement of people and basic foodstuffs; photo (M)

2000-10-22.title: Hindu Hard-Liners Pressing To Keep Excluding Muslims
2000-10-22.abstract: Hindu nationalist hard-liners continue pressing anti-Muslim policies, despite efforts by new party president Bangaru Laxman to distanceparty from chauvinist thinking; photos (M)

2000-10-22.title: Ukrainian Says He Arrested Wallenberg
2000-10-22.abstract: Elderly Ukrainian named Bogdan Tarnavsky claims in interview that he was anti-Nazi fighter who arrested Raoul Wallenberg, legendary Swedish diplomat who helped thousands of Jews escape Nazis before vanishing in Soviet hands in 1945; Tarnavsky, whose story differs from accepted account that Wallenberg drove off with Soviet guards, says he was stopped Wallenberg in Hungarian countryside and brought him back to his partisan unit; says intelligence operatives had Wallenberg whisked onto airplane to Lviv and then Moscow that night (S)

2000-10-22.title: 150 Nations Move to Curb Tobacco Use
2000-10-22.abstract: Officials representing nearly 150 governments lay groundwork at World Health Organization summit in Geneva, Switzerland, for new global treaty to control tobacco use and stamp out adolescent smoking; agency is pushing for strong international accord with goal of finalizing treaty by 2003 (M)

2000-10-23.title: Barak Seeks To Heal Rift With Inquiry And New Aid
2000-10-23.abstract: Prime Min Ehud Barak moves to heal rift with Israel's Arab citizens by announcing inquiry into deaths of 13 Israeli Arabs in current unrest and unveiling four-year development plan for Arab communities; move is met with skepticism among Israeli Arab leaders, who say inquiry, by committee of experts and retired judges instead of official state commission, will lack binding authority; say Barak's plan fails to address roots of inequality of Israel's one million Arab citizens (S)

2000-10-23.title: Investigators Discouraging Speculation In Cole Attack
2000-10-23.abstract: American officials caution against conclusions that they are close to identifying group responsible or to retaliating militarily for bombing of destroyer Cole in Aden, Yemen, despite new clues suggesting that attack may have involved more than two men who died in blast; Yemeni officials working with US investigators report discovery of three more safe houses, bringing total discovered so far to five; Yemeni security sources say group of officials have been detained for allegedly allowing government offices to issue identity documents with assumed names used in conspiracy; photo (M)

2000-10-23.title: Chinese Official Beats Albright to North Korea
2000-10-23.abstract: China's defense minister, Chi Haotian, arrives in North Korea hours before of US Sec of State Madeleine K Albright; is accompanied by senior military officials for five-day visit (S)

2000-10-23.title: Beijing Journal; Chinese Scholar Learns the Danger of Criticizing
2000-10-23.abstract: Chinese authorities are working to muffle Yu Jie, 25-year-old scholar whose writings, notably 1998 book Fire and Ice, have denounced country's official culture as vacuous; he has been denied job at state-controlled Chinese Modern Literature Institute, and appears to be effectively blackballed from paying position as scholar; faces expulsion to his small hometown in Sichuan Province, since he is ineligible for Beijing residency certificate unless he secures job; travails exemplify tighter constraints on many of country's livelier thinkers, who have not courted prison with direct activism, but whose careers are in jeopardy because they write so pointedly about China's shortcomings; photo (M)

2000-10-23.title: With Economy Booming, Canadian Leader Calls for Elections
2000-10-23.abstract: Prime Min Jean Chretien calls for new elections in Canada, apparently counting on booming economy and popular new tax-cutting package to make him nation's first leader since World War II to win third consecutive mandate (M)

2000-10-24.title: Lebanese Businessman Once Again is Premier
2000-10-24.abstract: Rafik Hariri, Lebanese billionaire, is named Lebanon's Prime Minister by Pres Emile Lahoud; he and supporters won landsilde victory in parliamentary elections in August (S)

2000-10-24.title: Barak and Sharon Open Talks on Forming a Coalition
2000-10-24.abstract: Prime Minister Ehud Barak of Israel formally opens talks with Ariel Sharon, rightist opposition leader, on forming coalition government, but talks recess for day after first disagreements crop up; Barak, who has lost majority in Parliament, hopes move will enable him to escape vote by lawmakers for early elections and win broad public support for his policies in confrontation with Palestinians; Sharon is seeking veto on future concessions in any peace talks; would improve political chances against main rightist rival, former Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu, by entering cabinet; Barak argues current wave of Palestinian protests and rising tensions on Israel's border with Lebanon require broad-based government; Israeli peace advocates and Palestinian officials warn bringing Sharon into government will effectively bury any hope of peace, sending final signal that Barak has given up on negotiations; guns battles erupt between Israeli troops and Palestiniana erupt again on edge of Jerusalem, with no end in sight to violence; photo (M)

2000-10-24.title: Sybil Milton, 59, Scholar of Nazis and Holocaust
2000-10-24.abstract: Dr Sybil H Milton, leading scholar of Nazi Germany and the Holocaust and senior historian at United States Holocaust Memorial Museum in Washington, dies at age 59 (M)

2000-10-24.title: Intelligence Chief Returns, Sending Peru Into Disarray
2000-10-24.abstract: Former intelligence chief Vladimiro Montesinos returns to Peru, month after fleeing for asylum in Panama, plunging government of Pres Alberto K Fujimori into disarray; First Vice Pres Francisco Tudela, close ally of Mr. Fujimori, resigns; police clash with protestors in downtown Lima; opposition leaders, fearing coup attempt by Montesinos and military high command, pledge to take to streets; Washington warns any attempt to disrupt constitutional order will lead to Peru's isolation; reasons behind Montesinos's return remain unclear; arrived in Peru without official flight plan; landed at air force base in Pisco after officials at airport in Lima refused landing permission; government officials respond with surprise and confusion; Fujimori is silent; summons cabinet and military commanders; later visits military and intelligence installations around Lima; military analysts characterize Montesinos's landing as direct challenge to Fujimori's authority by military; photos (M)

2000-10-24.title: With General Trailing, Ivory Coast Vote Tally Suddenly Dries Up
2000-10-24.abstract: Results of October 22 Ivory Coast presidential election are delayed without explanation after early results show junta leader, Gen Robert Guei, trailing main opponent Laurent Gbagbo by 11 percentage points; Ivoirians express fears military government may be manipulating outcome (M)

2000-10-25.title: Totalitarian Chic Reigns in Spotless Pyongyang
2000-10-25.abstract: Scene in Pyongyan, North Korea, described; streets are spotlessly clean; women and children wear brightly hued clothes, many of them apparently sewn at home; reporters are denied access to countryside, where life reportedly is far harsher; photo (M)

2000-10-25.title: Bear Market Encourages Alternative Investments
2000-10-25.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  The challenge for any investor in a bear market is to escape the galloping herd: thousands of people reacting to the same bad news by hitting sell buttons within seconds of one another.

2000-10-25.title: Ivory Coast Ruler Declares Himself Winner
2000-10-25.abstract: Gen Robert Guei declares self winner of presidential election, dissolving electoral commission that showed him trailing opposition leader Laurent Gbagbo and halting vote counting; move stirs widespread protests in Abidjan and several other cities; Gbagbo urges supporters to stand up against Guei; security forces seem to remain loyal to Guei; center of Abidjan is quiet at night after government declares state of emergency and imposes nighttime curfew throughout nation; European Union, including France, and United States score Geui move; photo (M)

2000-10-25.title: No Special Alert for Cole Before Bombing
2000-10-25.abstract: Navy says destroyer Cole was at medium-level alert, which is routinely in force in Persian Gulf, when it was bombed in Aden, Yemen, despite general warning of possible terrorist attack on American warship; says there were only handful of sailors with rifles mounting watch on deck as ship entered harbor and no boats guarding waters around ship as flotilla of service boats moved in to aid with mooring and refueling; Yemeni security officials confirm finding house overlooking harbor from which conspirators apparently monitored Navy ships entering Aden and possibly directed attack (M)

2000-10-25.title: Clinton Urges Arafat to Calm Rioting in the West Bank
2000-10-25.abstract: Pres Clinton calls Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat to urge him to bring rioting in West Bank and Gaza under control quickly; gets no assurances and no response to invitation for Arafat to visit United States soon to map way back to peace talks; plans to speak promptly with Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak, with similar call for restraint and invitation to separate meeting; Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin also calls Arafat to urge restraint, after hearing from Barak; Palestinian rioting abates somewhat; Israeli military officials expect violence to continue for months; say nature of conflict is changing from street battles to low-level warfare; photo (M)

2000-10-26.title: Fujimori Orders Military To Arrest His Former Spy Chief
2000-10-26.abstract: Pres Alberto K Fujimori orders military units to hunt down former intelligence chief, Vladimiro Montesinos, who has returned to Peru from monthlong exile in Panama; Fujimori suggests that elements of security forces probably helped Montesinos escape; Montesinos, who had been Fujimori's closest aide, is suspected of committing variety of crimes involving corruption and human rights violations; it is not clear exactly what Fujimori hopes to gain from search operation for Montesinos; photo (M)

2000-10-26.title: $100 Million Voted for Serbia, but With War-Crimes Strings
2000-10-26.abstract: Congress approves $100 million aid package to Serbia, but makes it contingent on new Yugoslav government's cooperation in apprehending those indicted on charges of war crimes, including former Pres Slobodan Milosevic; Congressional experts acknowledge it may be difficult for Yugoslavia's new president, Vojislav Kostunica, and his deputies to comply with some of provisions (M)

2000-10-26.title: Clinton Almost Blames Arafat for Not Controlling Violence
2000-10-26.abstract: Pres Clinton comes close to blaming Yasir Arafat, Palestinian leader, for failing to rein in violence, and he offers some of his warmest praise of Israeli Prime Min Barak; Clinton is trying to have both men visit Washington for separate consultations on restarting peace effort; White House officials say privately that any chance of resuming peace talks would end if Barak forms emergency government with Ariel Sharon, hard-line opposition leader; American officials fear that any seeming meddling in internal affairs of Israel would weaken Barak (M)

2000-10-26.title: Yugoslavia, Turning a Page, Is Expected to Rejoin United Nations
2000-10-26.abstract: American ambassador to United Nations, Richard Holbrooke, says Yugoslavia will soon apply to join UN, indicating clear change in policy under its new president, Vojislav Kostunica; Holbrooke is first prominent member of Clinton cabinet to meet with Kostunica; says Kostunica said he is working on releasing 800 Kosovo Albanians being held in Serb prisons, and suggests that law granting them amnesty is possibility (M)

2000-10-26.title: Reporter's Notebook; Poverty, Paradox and a 'Dear Leader'
2000-10-26.abstract: Reporter's Notebook vignettes from Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright's visit to North Korea to meet with Kim Jong Il (M)

2000-10-27.title: Suicide Attack in Gaza Strip Increases Tension in Mideast
2000-10-27.abstract: Palestinian suicide bomber, heading on his bicycle toward an Israeli Army outpost in Gaza Strip, succeeds only in killing himself but raises anxiety in Israel that violence is shifting into new phase; Islamic Jihad claims responsibility for attack by Nabil al-Arair; photos (M)

2000-10-27.title: Playing Cat and Mouse, With Copters, in Peru
2000-10-27.abstract: Peruvian Pres Alberto K Fujimori is in apparent high-stakes chase after his spy chief Valdimiro Montesinos; Fujimore spends much of day in police helicopter directing manhunt against former aide, who is said to be at large in suburbs of Lima; all members of armed forces who are not involved in operation have been ordered to stay in their barracks, suggesting that Fujimori does not have full confidence in his army; search leads many people to wonder what is really going on, and some observers suspect search is really coordinated farce, saying Fujimoro's current relationship with Montesinos is unclear (M)

2000-10-27.title: Dictator Gone, Violence Erupts In Ivory Coast
2000-10-27.abstract: Residents of Ivory Coast who came together to topple military dictator now turn against one another in series of deadly clashes; violence between supporters of two of main political parties is sparked by religious and ethnic difference and leaders' ambitions; it begins in capital city Abidjan and spreads to smaller cities; winner of disputed election, Laurent Gbagbo, is sworn in as president, with backing of security forces; he rebuffs calls for new election from country's two major parities, United Nations, United States and Organization for African Unity; Alassane D Ouattara, former prime minister who was barred from running in election, takes refuge in German ambassador's residence after security forces surround his house and fire tear gas and ammunition; Ouattara claims attack was led by supporters of Gbagbo and backed by paramilitary gendarmes; photo (M)

2000-10-27.title: Report Calls Officials to Task For Having Misled the Public : U.K. Understated 'Mad Cow' Threat
2000-10-27.abstract: LONDON—  An official investigation of Britain's "mad cow" crisis criticized government officials Thursday for misleading the public with a "campaign of reassurance," saying they played down potential risks to human health for years in order to prevent consumer panic and protect the country's beef industry.

2000-10-27.title: Anti-U.S. Fever: Arab Bitterness and Boycott Talk
2000-10-27.abstract: Militant tone of unalloyed hostility toward Israel and deep distrust of US policy continues in state-controlled media of Arab countries, mirroring flood of public sympathy and political activity in support of Palestinians; reminders of the conflict are everywhere, stirring sense of pan-Arab solidarity, and impact on regional politics could be substantial; attitudes toward United States are also hardening, and there is talk of boycotting US products (M)

2000-10-28.title: World Briefing
2000-10-28.abstract: THE AMERICAS    ARGENTINA: SEEKS TO EXTRADITE PINOCHET  A judge asked Chile's Supreme Court to extradite the former dictator Augusto Pinochet for the killing of a former Chilean army chief, Carlos Prats, and his wife in Buenos Aires in 1974. General Pinochet, 84, is already fighting a possible trial in Chile for rights abuses during his 17-year rule. He returned home this year after 503 days under arrest in Britain. General Prats, who opposed General Pinochet's coup against President Salvador Allende in 1973, was killed by a car bomb.    (Reuters)

2000-10-28.title: Canada Arrests 2 in 1985 Bombing of Jet
Taiwan Ends Construction of Its 4th Nuclear Plant
Israeli Troops Shoot 4 Palestinians Dead in New Violence
In Rural China, a Steep Price Of Poverty: Dying of AIDS
2000-10-28.abstract: Doctors say that small towns in Henan Province are experiencing AIDS epidemic; Chinese officials deny there is AIDS in Henan; prohibit news media and government health workers from discussing issue and block outside researchers from studying it; covert studies suggest that Henan has some of highest localized rates of HIV infection in world; doctors say that poor farmers have illegally sold their blood to people whose unsterile collection methods have left many infected with HIV virus; map; photo (M)

2000-10-29.title: Fujimori Fires 3 Top Aides In Military
Yemen Restricting F.B.I. Access to Suspects in Cole Bombing
2000-10-29.abstract: Yemen reportedly is barring Federal Bureau of Investigation agents probing bombing of destroyer Cole from questioning suspects or witnesses, attending interviews with them or moving at will around Aden to follow clues, raising fears that evidence-gathering may be delayed or suppressed; Pres Clinton, State Sec Madeleine K Albright and FBI Dir Louis J Freeh have appealed for broader cooperation; FBI has moved its remaining 40 to 50 agents in Aden to Navy ships at sea outside city; some 100 others have returned home; photo (M)

2000-10-29.title: 3 Killed in Trench Collapse
2000-10-29.abstract: RICHMOND, Texas -- A construction trench collapsed Saturday, killing three workers who were buried in 14 feet of dirt, authorities said.

2000-10-29.title: Behind Colombia's Election Hoopla, Rebels Wield Power
2000-10-29.abstract: Colombia's villages are swept by campaign festivities in advance of nationwide municipal elections; Marxist guerrillas have been active for months in perhaps half of villages, quietly manipulating electoral process by giving their tacit approval to some candidates, forcing some to withdraw and assassinating few others; effort compromises nation's fragile democracy and strengthens position of Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia, nation's largest rebel movement; paramilitary gunmen have struck back, killing candidates believed to be rebel sympathizers; map; photo (M)

2000-10-30.title: Barak Gets a Breather as He Tries to Form a Unity Government
2000-10-30.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak fails to forge emergency unity government with right wing; is unlikely to face immediate dismissal when Parliament reconvenes on October 30 after three-month recess; ultra-Orthodox Shas Party, which pulled out of government before Camp David peace talks in July, says it will grant Barak temporary 'safety net' while violence continues to rage between Israeli troops and Palestinians; three more Palestinians die in confrontations in West Bank; Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat says people will remain steadfast in struggle (M)

2000-10-30.title: Investigation of Attack on U.S. Destroyer Moving Slowly
2000-10-30.abstract: American investigators still lack concrete evidence in bombing of destroyer USS Cole in Aden harbor in Yemen, despite trail of clues suggesting ship was bombed by Islamic militants, possibly associated with Osama bin Laden; say they are being slowed by Yemeni officials' refusal to allow questioning of scores of people detained so far; Clinton administration seems content to give investigators time to build stronger case before retailating, which could defer potentially divisive question of military response until after November 7 election (M)

2000-10-30.title: Italy's New Politics: The Beauty Contest
2000-10-30.abstract: Campaigning for Italy's 2001 parliamentary elections, which will determine whether country stays center-left or veers to center-right, is fixated on looks; Silvio Berlusconi, media tycoon and leader of center-right Sforza Italia, is perennially tanned with glossy good looks and Continental charm; Francesco Rutelli, 46-year-old Mayor of Rome, evinces dash of Paul Newman glamour; is hailed as having face that would not look out of place among busts of Roman emperors in Capitoline museums; each candidate claims to be face of new Italy, modern democracy shaking itself loose from lingering economic rigidity, red tape and corruption to meet challenges of global economy; photos (M)

2000-10-30.title: U.S. Braces for Trouble in Indonesia as Relations Rupture
2000-10-30.abstract: Relations between the United States and Indonesia have deteriorated rapidly after a series of high-profile disputes between the American ambassador here and Indonesian officials and lawmakers, who have accused the United States of meddling.     Apparently as a result, protests and threats against American citizens, companies and facilities are on the rise in this country, which has the world's fourth-largest population and the largest Muslim population. Fearing what it called a ''credible threat of attack,'' the United States Embassy in Jakarta has closed its doors to the public for the foreseeable future.

2000-10-30.title: Venezuelan Leader Finds a Teammate in Castro
2000-10-30.abstract: Venezuela's Pres Hugo Chavez and visiting Cuban Pres Fidel Castro hold four-hour gabfest on Chavez's weekly radio program in Caracas; take turns criticizing world order dominated by US, answer phone calls and even sing song together; photo (M)

2000-10-31.title: Family Doctors In Netherlands Stage a Protest
2000-10-31.abstract: Family doctors in Netherlands stage protest in the Hague, charging they are overworked and underpaid; charge Health Ministry with ignoring urgent pleas to reduce 70-hour weeks and for more subsidies to hire extra staff members because demands on primary care physicians are expanding year after year (S)

2000-10-31.title: 6 Dead After Italian Military Helicopter Crashes
2000-10-31.abstract: LEGHORN, Italy -- An Italian military police helicopter crashed off a Tuscan island overnight, killing at least six officers. The search was resuming Tuesday morning for the other two people aboard.

2000-10-31.title: The Desperate Risk Death in a Desert
2000-10-31.abstract: Sasabe, Mexico, is one of newest and fastest growing gateways to US for illegal Mexican immigrants as border enforcement is stepped up, particularly in cities; desert region on Arizona border is one of most inhospitable in North Americ, known for fierce winter storms and soaring temperatures; Immigration and Naturalization Service reports 369 immigrants have died trying to cross border since October 1999 and that 100 of deaths occurred along Arizona border--up from 19 deaths in 1998; map; photo (M)

2000-10-31.title: Ship Attack Suspects Seemed Out of Place
2000-10-31.abstract: Neighbors recall two Arab strangers suspected in bombing of US destroyer Cole in port of Aden, Yemen, seemed out of place but were never incongruous, after renting apartment on hillside overlooking port on June 9; neighbors cite their their thick accents, Islamic-style dress, seeming obsession with privacy, frugality with food they bought in local market and curious comings and goings in dead of night; say men described themselves as fish merchants, but recall they were never seen in local market; say neither went to prayers in new, whitewashed mosque that is pride of neighborhood; eyewitness descriptions of men who boarded boat matches exactly appearance, accents and clothing of those who lived in rented apartment; neighbors are convinced men and suicide bombers are same people; ramming of destroyer on October 12 during refueling stop killed 17 soldiers and wounded 39 others; map; photos (M)

2000-10-31.title: Navy Ships Avoiding Suez Canal Since Destroyer Bombing
2000-10-31.abstract: US Navy ships are ordered to avoid Suez Canal; order comes in wake of bombing of destroyer Cole during refueling stop in Aden, Yemen, on October 12 in which 17 soldiers died (S)
